title: mammillaria heyderi subsp. coahuilensis
intro: Mammillaria heyderi subsp. coahuilensis is a rare and endangered species of cactus, native to the Mexican state of Coahuila, belonging to the Mammillaria genus. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Mammillaria heyderi subsp. coahuilensis include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with infections and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use requires caution and proper handling due to potential toxicity.
